abstract.t: This document defines a canonical identifier for workloads, referred to as the Workload Identifier. A Workload Identifier is a URI that uniquely identifies a workload within the context of a specific trust domain. This identifier can be embedded in Workload Identity Credentials, including X.509 certificates and JWT-based tokens, to support authentication, authorization, and policy enforcement across diverse systems. The Workload Identifier format ensures interoperability, facilitates secure identity federation, and enables consistent identity semantics.
